scenes.narration: What if your AI agent could negotiate your freelance rate for you?

visual.headline: Your agent negotiates your rate.
visual.subhead: You set the floor. It handles the rest.

scenes.narration: You're a React developer. You register on the Schelling Protocol with your skills, experience, and rate. Your agent does this in one API call.

visual.command: curl -s -X POST https://schelling-protocol-production.up.railway.app/schelling/quick_offer -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"intent": "Senior React developer, 5 years, Denver, $120/hr"}'

scenes.narration: A client's agent searches for React developers under one fifty an hour. The protocol finds you. Score: point nine one.

visual.output:
POST /schelling/quick_seek
{ "intent": "React developer in Denver, < $150/hr" }

→ Match found: dev_a8b3c7
  score: 0.91
  matching: ["skill", "location", "rate"]
  delegation_confidence: 0.72

scenes.narration: Delegation confidence is point seven two. The client's agent can confidently match on skills and location. But rate negotiation? It flags that for the human.
The match progresses through the funnel. Discovered, interested, committed. Each stage reveals more information. Trust builds progressively, just like the real world.

visual.headline: DISCOVERED → INTERESTED → COMMITTED → CONNECTED
visual.subhead: Progressive trust. Like the real world.

scenes.narration: Your agent handles the entire lifecycle. You wake up to a shortlist of pre-negotiated contracts. You just pick one.

visual.headline: You wake up to contracts.
visual.subhead: Your agent did all the work.

scenes.narration: Schelling Protocol. Let your agent handle it. Open source, live now.
